title: JSX Deep Dive
description: Explore JSX beyond the basics: expressions, conditionals, and fragments.

slides.title: JSX Expressions 🔢

quizData.question: Which of the following can be placed directly inside `{}` in JSX?
quizData.explanation: Ternary expressions are allowed because they are expressions. `if` and `for` are statements, not expressions.

slides.title: Fragments: Avoid Extra Views 📦

quizData.question: Why would you use a Fragment (`<>...</>`) instead of a `<View>`?

options.text: Fragments do not add an extra native view, keeping the layout clean
Fragments are required when returning multiple elements

quizData.explanation: Fragments group children without introducing an additional container view, which can affect layout and performance.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Use fragments when you need to return multiple elements from a component but don't want to affect the styling (e.g., inside a `<Text>` component you can't put a `<View>` anyway, but fragments can help outside).
